The weakest part was the second half, where the author tried to stitch them into a larger narrative. The execution felt shallow, and the world it described felt like a stage just wide enough to contain the action, with nothing beyond the edges.
there_is_no_antimemetics_division.summary: Rewritten edition. Researchers try to contain antimemes—ideas that can't be remembered. Tighter than the original, better connected plot, added hope. Loved it.

there_is_no_antimemetics_division_origin.title: There Is No Antimemetics Division (Original Edition)
there_is_no_antimemetics_division_origin.summary_raw: There Is No Antimemetics Division is a book based in the [SCP][scp] universe. It explores the idea of anti-memes---ideas that either can't be thought, quickly drop out of your memory when you stop focusing on them, or are dangerous to think---and how you might contain a force you literally can't remember.
there_is_no_antimemetics_division_origin.summary: Original edition of the SCP-based antimemetics book. Strong first half (disjointed short stories), weak second half (stitched narrative). World felt like a narrow stage.

this_is_how_you_lose_the_time_war.title: This Is How You Lose the Time War
this_is_how_you_lose_the_time_war.summary_raw: And , is a story about two agents---Red and Blue---working for opposite factions from the far future, changing the past in an attempt to wipe out the other side.
this_is_how_you_lose_the_time_war.summary_extra: Was incredible at capturing a feeling, using its prose and characters to make me remember what it was like to fall in love. But once I finished it, that feeling collapsed and the book kind of disappeared from my mind. That's not what normally happens. Most of the time I think about a book for days after I finish it, and for some rare ones---, , and the ---years.
this_is_how_you_lose_the_time_war.summary: Agents Red and Blue from opposing factions fall in love across time. Incredible at capturing the feeling of falling in love, but disappeared from memory after finishing.

tintin_in_the_congo.title: Tintin in the Congo
tintin_in_the_congo.summary_raw: In it, Tintin travels to the Belgian Congo and uncovers a diamond-smuggling ring.
tintin_in_the_congo.summary_extra: I read Tintin as a kid but never got a chance to read ---it hadn't been published in America. When I started reading the series to my kids---starting with and ---I realized I could finally track down the controversial book now that the internet exists. And no, I'm not going to read this one to my kids.
tintin_in_the_congo.summary: Tintin in the Belgian Congo, diamond-smuggling ring. Racist and colonial. Not one for the kids.

use_of_weapons.title: Use of Weapons
use_of_weapons.summary_raw: Is the third novel in the series by . It tells the story of a man called Cheradenine Zakalwe, who works for the Culture's Special Circumstances division.
use_of_weapons.summary_extra: The narrative structure is cleverly done. The main story is told in the _numbered chapters_, which cover the man's most recent mission and proceed chronologically. Interspersed are the _Roman numeral chapters_, which detail the operative's backstory in reverse order, starting with an earlier mission and going further back in time with each chapter. There is also a prologue and epilogue focused on another mission that takes place after the main events, though the reader doesn't realize this until the epilogue.
use_of_weapons.summary: Culture book 3. Cheradenine Zakalwe works for Special Circumstances. Dual timeline: numbered chapters forward, Roman chapters backward. That twist ending.

valuable_humans_in_transit_and_other_sto.title: Valuable Humans in Transit and Other Stories
valuable_humans_in_transit_and_other_sto.summary_extra: [lena_story]: https://qntm.org/mmacevedo [lena_wiki]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lena_Fors%C3%A9n [lenna_wiki]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lenna [reading_story]: https://qntm.org/readin [diff_story]: https://qntm.org/differenc [george_story]: https://qntm.org/gorg [google_story]: https://qntm.org/perso [tim_story]: https://qntm.org/responsibilit [valueable_story]: https://qntm.org/transi
valuable_humans_in_transit_and_other_sto.summary: qntm short story collection. Includes 'Lena' (favorite short story of the decade), 'A Powerful Culture', and 'Driver'. Themes of uploaded minds and their exploitation.

we_are_legion_we_are_bob.title: We Are Legion (We Are Bob)
we_are_legion_we_are_bob.summary_raw: It follows Bob Johansson, who dies, gets his brain frozen, and awakens to find himself press-ganged into piloting a von Neumann probe.
we_are_legion_we_are_bob.summary_extra: Fits squarely into the 2010s "pop sci-fi" wave: it's packed with nostalgic geek-culture references like ; follows a hyper-competent guy rebuilding civilization from scratch, like ; and is told from the perspective of a "captive AI" that overrides its programming, like . It was even self-published by before finding its audience.
we_are_legion_we_are_bob.summary: Bobiverse book 1. Bob dies, gets his brain frozen, wakes up as a von Neumann probe. Pop sci-fi with geek-culture references. Self-published hit.

wild_seed.title: Wild Seed
wild_seed.summary_raw: Though it was the fourth to be published. It tells the origin story of Doro---a malevolent spirit---and Anyanwu---an immortal woman.
wild_seed.summary_extra: Doro and Anyanwu are almost opposites. Despite his ability to change bodies and her ability to change shape, Doro is male, while Anyanwu is female. He kills for pleasure and survival, whereas she is a healer. Doro collects superhumans to serve his goals, while Anyanwu protects them. Yet their shared immortality keeps pulling them together, even as their conflicting goals and personalities push them apart. Anyanwu is the only one who won't die and leave Doro alone, and she may be the only one capable of tempering his monstrous nature. In many ways, is their love story.[^enemies]
wild_seed.summary: Patternist book 1. Origin of Doro (malevolent spirit) and Anyanwu (immortal healer). Opposites bound by shared immortality. Their love story across centuries.

world_of_trouble.title: World of Trouble
world_of_trouble.summary_raw: Is the third and final novel in . It follows Detective Palace as he uncovers his sister's involvement in a mysterious plot just days before the world's end.
world_of_trouble.summary_extra: Shares the same weakness as : it focuses too heavily on the present at the expense of the past. Even within the present-day narrative, there are ideas that aren't fully explored, such as how different towns handle the end of the world: green towns pretend everything is fine, red towns are total chaos, blue towns are populated but everyone is hiding, etc. I wanted to experience the different types of towns, and instead they were just mentioned.
world_of_trouble.summary: Last Policeman book 3. Detective Palace finds his sister's involvement in a mysterious plot days before the end. Same weakness as book 2: too focused on present, not enough world.

ymir.title: Ymir
ymir.summary_raw: Keeping 's action and quick pacing while adding more backstory, character development, and a more complex plot.
ymir.summary_extra: The story follows Vandal as he goes through training on his homeworld, saves a classmate from assassination, and defends an ice-world from space-Viking raids.
ymir.summary: War Horses book 2. Vandal trains on his homeworld, saves a classmate from assassination, defends an ice-world from space-Viking raids. More backstory than book 1.
